title: The Hidden Value of Talking to Strangers
subtitle: 和陌生人说话：被低估的日常幸福源泉

paragraphs.en: Most of us assume warmth and belonging come mainly from friends and family. Yet commuter studies show that brief conversations with strangers can improve mood and social connectedness. The benefit appears even in ordinary settings like buses and trains.
paragraphs.zh: 我们大多数人认为温暖与归属感主要来自朋友和家人。然而，针对通勤者的研究表明，与陌生人的简短交谈也能改善情绪并增强社会联结感。这种益处甚至在公交车和地铁这样的日常场景中也会出现。

paragraphs.en: Participants asked to talk with nearby strangers reported better experiences than those told to remain silent. A major barrier is prediction error, because people often overestimate rejection risk before initiating contact. In practice, many strangers are more willing to engage than we expect.
paragraphs.zh: 被要求主动与身旁陌生人交谈的参与者，其体验报告普遍好于被要求保持沉默的人。一个主要障碍在于预测偏差——人们在开启对话前，往往会高估被拒绝的风险。而实际上，许多陌生人比我们预想的更愿意回应。

paragraphs.en: This does not mean every interaction must become deep conversation. It means small social risks can yield disproportionate psychological gains in everyday life. Reframing strangers as potential micro-connections can make public spaces feel less isolating.
paragraphs.zh: 这并不意味着每次互动都要发展成深度的交谈。它只是说明，在日常生活中，微小的社交风险可能换来不成比例的心理收益。将陌生人重新定义为潜在的微联结对象，能让公共空间变得不那么令人孤立。
